ddls:
CREATE TABLE "workclasses" (
  "workclass_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each workclass */,
  "workclass_name" TEXT /* Name of the workclass */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the workclass */,
  "average_hours_per_week" INTEGER /* Average hours worked per week for the workclass */,
  "median_income" INTEGER /* Median income for the workclass */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("workclass_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "educations" (
  "education_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each education level */,
  "education_name" TEXT /* Name of the education level */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the education level */,
  "average_educational_num" INTEGER /* Average number of years of education for the education level */,
  "employment_rate" REAL /* Employment rate for the education level */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("education_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "marital_statuses" (
  "marital_status_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each marital status */,
  "marital_status_name" TEXT /* Name of the marital status */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the marital status */,
  "average_household_size" INTEGER /* Average household size for the marital status */,
  "average_income" INTEGER /* Average income for the marital status */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("marital_status_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "occupations" (
  "occupation_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each occupation */,
  "occupation_name" TEXT /* Name of the occupation */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the occupation */,
  "average_hours_per_week" INTEGER /* Average hours worked per week for the occupation */,
  "median_income" INTEGER /* Median income for the occupation */,
  "unemployment_rate" REAL /* Unemployment rate for the occupation */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("occupation_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "relationships" (
  "relationship_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each relationship status */,
  "relationship_name" TEXT /* Name of the relationship status */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the relationship status */,
  "average_household_size" INTEGER /* Average household size for the relationship status */,
  "average_income" INTEGER /* Average income for the relationship status */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("relationship_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "races" (
  "race_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each race */,
  "race_name" TEXT /* Name of the race */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the race */,
  "average_income" INTEGER /* Average income for the race */,
  "unemployment_rate" REAL /* Unemployment rate for the race */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("race_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "genders" (
  "gender_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each gender */,
  "gender_name" TEXT /* Name of the gender */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the gender */,
  "average_income" INTEGER /* Average income for the gender */,
  "unemployment_rate" REAL /* Unemployment rate for the gender */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("gender_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "native_countries" (
  "native_country_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each native country */,
  "native_country_name" TEXT /* Name of the native country */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the native country */,
  "average_income" INTEGER /* Average income for individuals from the native country */,
  "immigration_rate" REAL /* Immigration rate for the native country */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("native_country_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., researcher, data analyst, admin) */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password for the user (hashed) */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Last login timestamp */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the user account was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the user account was last updated */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Whether the user account is active */,
  "is_admin" INTEGER /* Whether the user has admin privileges */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "industries" (
  "industry_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each industry */,
  "industry_name" TEXT /* Name of the industry */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the industry */,
  "average_income" INTEGER /* Average income for the industry */,
  "unemployment_rate" REAL /* Unemployment rate for the industry */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("industry_id")
)
